mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals an expansion in aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 90 signals a supply-side shock, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 90 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 5% signals a labor market downturn, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income (RSAFS) above 400 signals an expansion in aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising RSAFS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 2% signals an easing monetary cycle, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 95 signals a supply-side shock, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 95 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) below 4% signals a labor market upturn, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 4% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income (RSAFS) above 400 and a 2-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) above 500 signals an expansion in aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising RSAFS and HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
